questions.question: Do vaccines cause food allergies?
questions.rationale: Directly addresses a specific vaccine misconception that leads to dangerous vaccine avoidance behavior
questions.misinformation_narrative: Anti-vaccine groups and some parents believe that vaccines cause food allergies, citing the increase in both vaccine schedules and food allergy prevalence as evidence of causation
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy based on false allergy concerns undermines herd immunity and puts children at risk of preventable diseases

questions.question: Should parents avoid giving peanuts to infants to prevent allergies?
questions.rationale: Corrects persistent parenting misconception with major health consequences, backed by landmark LEAP trial evidence
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents still believe they should delay introducing peanuts and other allergens until after age 1-2 to prevent allergies, despite updated medical guidance
questions.public_stakes: Delayed allergen introduction may actually increase allergy risk, leading to more severe food allergies and associated health complications

questions.question: Are IgG food sensitivity tests accurate for diagnosing food allergies?
questions.rationale: Addresses a widespread commercial testing scam that causes real nutritional harm and medical confusion
questions.misinformation_narrative: Alternative medicine practitioners and online companies market IgG food sensitivity tests as reliable ways to identify food allergies and intolerances, leading people to unnecessarily restrict diets
questions.public_stakes: False positive results lead to unnecessary dietary restrictions, nutritional deficiencies, and delayed proper medical care for real food allergies

nasem_sources_preview.name: Reproducibility and Replicability in Science (2019)
Safety of Dietary Supplements for Horses, Dogs, and Cats (2009)
Progress in Preventing Childhood Obesity: How Do We Measure Up? (2007)
Preventing Childhood Obesity: Health in the Balance (2005)

questions.question: Are GLP-1 medications like Ozempic safe for weight loss?
questions.rationale: Addresses both overuse and underuse of important diabetes/obesity medications due to contradictory misinformation
questions.misinformation_narrative: Social media influencers and online pharmacies promote off-label GLP-1 use while others spread fears about dangerous side effects, creating confusion about legitimate medical use
questions.public_stakes: Inappropriate use without medical supervision causes harm while legitimate patients may avoid beneficial treatment due to safety fears

nasem_sources_preview.name: The Challenge of Treating Obesity and Overweight: Proceedings of a Workshop (2018)

questions.question: Is Narcan (naloxone) safe for non-medical people to use during overdoses?
questions.rationale: Corrects life-or-death misconception that prevents bystander intervention in overdose emergencies
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people fear using naloxone because they believe it's dangerous for non-trained individuals or could cause legal liability
questions.public_stakes: Hesitation to use naloxone during overdoses directly leads to preventable overdose deaths

Advancing Therapeutic Development for Pain and Opioid Use Disorders Through Public-Private Partnerships: Proceedings of a Workshop (2018)

questions.question: Can people with egg allergies safely receive flu vaccines?
questions.rationale: Corrects outdated medical guidance that still circulates despite updated safety evidence
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people with egg allergies and their healthcare providers still believe flu vaccines are dangerous for egg-allergic individuals due to egg-based production methods
questions.public_stakes: Egg-allergic individuals avoid flu vaccination unnecessarily, increasing their risk of serious influenza complications

questions.question: Do people need to avoid gluten if they don't have celiac disease?
questions.rationale: Addresses widespread dietary misconception with nutritional and economic consequences
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe gluten is inherently harmful and follow gluten-free diets without medical need, thinking it's healthier for everyone
questions.public_stakes: Unnecessary gluten avoidance can lead to nutritional deficiencies and higher food costs, while masking actual celiac disease diagnosis

questions.question: Should parents worry about blood clots from COVID-19 vaccines in children?
questions.rationale: Corrects specific vaccine safety misconception that drives pediatric vaccine hesitancy
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some parents avoid COVID-19 vaccination for children due to exaggerated fears about blood clot risks, often conflating adult adenovirus vaccine issues with mRNA vaccines used in children
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy puts children at risk of COVID-19 complications while avoiding minimal actual risk

questions.question: Do most children outgrow food allergies?
questions.rationale: Corrects misconceptions about food allergy prognosis that affect daily management decisions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some parents believe all food allergies are lifelong while others assume children will definitely outgrow them, leading to either over-restriction or dangerous exposure
questions.public_stakes: Incorrect assumptions about allergy persistence affect dietary management, emergency preparedness, and quality of life decisions

questions.question: Are most medications given to children properly tested for pediatric safety?
questions.rationale: Addresses gap between public assumptions and reality of pediatric drug testing that affects treatment decisions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Parents may assume all medications prescribed to children have been specifically tested in pediatric populations, when many are used off-label based on adult data
questions.public_stakes: Parents may not understand the limitations of pediatric drug safety data, potentially missing important monitoring needs or avoiding beneficial treatments
